name: London Branch - The Stags Head
notesHtml:
Contact: If you're interested in joining the branch or travelling with us please contact Kevin Wells on 01279 416522 (Home), 01279 427431 (Work) or e-mail him (kwells@gwc-accountants.co.uk). Alternatively you can visit our website at www.londonfoxes.com.

Leicester City Supporters' Club (London Branch) was founded prior to the 1977-78 season and covers primarily London and its bordering counties.

Travel: The core activity of the club is centred round travelling together to home and away matches. Most journeys are by train with discounted trips being organised outside the Network South East region. A fast train to Leicester takes just over an hour (hopefully) from London St Pancras.

Social: The club has a darts team and we play against London Branches of other Supporters' Clubs.

name: Blue Army Sweden
notesHtml:
It began in 1969 when schoolmates Sören Filipsson and Kenneth Fredriksson started to support LCFC after they had been on TV in a game vs. Cardiff. The start of the supporters club was 1999 when Sören and Kenneth watched LCFC live in England for the first time (away at Wimbledon – a belter by Steve Guppy in 1-0 win). Now Blue Army Sweden have 22 members spread all over Sweden, from north to south. Most members live in Hallsberg, though.

Contact: Soren Filipsson

foxes.of.sweden@telia.com

http://www.bluearmysweden.com/

name: Foxes Pride
notesHtml:
Key contact/s: Graeme Smith (Chair), Gareth Miller (Secretary)

The objectives of Foxes Pride are:

- To enable its members to have a shared interest in Leicester City FC, and promote the Club within LGBT communities.
- To work cooperatively with other Leicester City FC supporters groups and other LGBT groups.
- To work with Leicester City FC and others to tackle homophobia, biphobia and transphobia in football.
- To promote the welfare of Leicester City FC's LGBT supporters, and ensure that King Power Stadium is a welcoming and friendly environment for all.

www.foxespride.co.uk
www.twitter.com/foxespride
www.facebook.com/foxespride

name: Oakham
notesHtml:
Home Games - We run coaches to all home games leaving the Railway Inn at 1pm for a 3pm kick off and 6pm for an evening kick off.

We pick up on route at Uppingham, Houghton on the hill and opposite Coles Nursery.

For further details and availability please contact Trevor Palmer on (01572) 756436 or by email to trevrailway85@btinternet.com or visit the Facebook group here: https://www.facebook.com/groups/150432844983482...

Away Games: We aim to run coaches to as many away games as possible.

name: Hinckley
notesHtml:
Away Games: Away travel can be booked on non-matchdays at Angela's, 50 New Street, Earl Shilton (01455) 848514 and on matchdays from the main club office at King Power Stadium, located on the ground floor in the South West corner of the ground.

Fans are advised to book early to avoid disappointment. Cash or cheques only please.

To view the Hinckley supporters club website, vist: http://www.lcschinckley.co.uk/

name: Ashby & Coalville
notesHtml:
To book away travel contact either Mick Bembridge on (01530) 811607 or Paddy Buckley on (01283) 223358.

Meetings take place on Sundays at the Blue Bell, Smisby Road, Ashby and every other Tuesday at the Snibsdon New Inn, Coalville. Everyone is made welcome.

name: Leicester travel
notesHtml:
Coaches are run to all away games. Bookings are taken in the Supporters Club Office (Match days). The office is situated in the West Stand opposite to the memorial gardens.

There is also a programme shop situated in this office. Programmes are on sale for City home and away games old and new.

Annual Membership
Adults: (new) £5 (renewals) £4
Juniors: (new and renewals) £2.

Phone 0116 274 2415 for more information.

name: Leicester City Supporters Club Scandinavian Branch (LCSCSB)
notesHtml:
Established:
1994

Public web page:
https://www.facebook.com/LCSCSB/

Contact:
Per Arne Hansen (Per.Arne.Hansen@notoddenfk.no)
Harald Venes (harald@venes.no)

The club's number of members is between 170-200 - mostly Norwegians, Danes and Swedes - but also a few English people living in Scandinavia.
The club has a newsletter released 3-4 times a year, a very active private Facebook-group and has good contacts within Leicester City as a football club.
Many of the members travel regularly to Leicester, and there are even a few season ticket holders in the club. They gather in pubs to watch Leicester City play in the biggest cities of Oslo, Bergen and Trondheim.

name: Cape Town Foxes
notesHtml:
The Cape Town Foxes was created by Philip Herbert, Bruce Nadin, Ex Chaplain for Leicester City Football Club for 4 years (2005 - 2009) and currently Chaplain for Ajax Cape Town Football Club, and Jason Taft (Uncle of George Taft, who was an academy player with LCFC) in 2011. We meet up regularly for the Leicester City games at Vasco Da Gama Tavern, which is mainly on Saturdays and during the week at Fireman's Arms for midweek games. We communicate meet-ups via Twitter and our Whatsapp group. We welcome all new supporters and any Leicester City supporters from around the world visiting our beautiful city of Cape Town.

Twitter: @CapeTownFoxes
Whatsapp Group: Cape Town Foxes
Contact: Philip Herbert

notesHtml:

OhioFoxesLCFC@gmail.com

Social Media:
Find us on Twitter - @OhioFoxes

Background:
The Ohio Foxes are a proud LCFC Supporters Group located in Columbus, Ohio, USA. Our goal is to connect and build lasting friendships with all Foxes fans across the great Buckeye state. We host meet-ups and watch-parties for both LCFC league and cup competitions at Fado Irish Pub in Columbus, Ohio.

name: Leicester City Türkiye
notesHtml:
Contact name: Eren Altun & Yalçın Kayra Koçer

Contact emails:

lcfc.tr@gmail.com
erenaltun42@gmail.com

Twitter: @LEICity_TR

We are two best friends from Istanbul, and we love to watch Premier League games every week. We realise that we have love inside our heart about Leicester City. So we decided to start a Twitter account. Our aim was having a nice community to talk with other people who love Leicester City in Turkey. We are sharing live matchday comments, pre and post-match discussion, funny posts, transfer rumours, questions like polls, injury reports and many more. We have tweeted more than 3400 tweets. It has been 10 months since we started, and we have 1460 followers now. Followers are so active every day, and we can communicate with them very easily. Our community also grows so fast. Last month our Twitter account got more than 90,000 views also our tweets get more than 800,000 views in total. This community grows so fast, and we are so happy about it. That is why we decided to be known by our club. We would like to show how Turkish people love Leicester City. As I mentioned before, we were just two best friends at the beginning but now we have more than 1,460 followers. And I am sure we will be one of the biggest Leicester City supporter group in a few years or less.
